name: Tortellini in Porcini Broth
recipeIngredient: 1 ounce dried Porcini or Polish mushrooms
4 ounces mixed wild and domestic mushrooms, trimmed and cut into thin slices
1 cup tortellini, stuffed with Porcini or plain cheese
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
Fresh finely cut chives for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. If you haven't soaked the Porcini in advance (see note below), place the Porcinis and water in a saucepan and bring to a simmer; let soak for 20 minutes or until tender.
2. Drain the soaked Porcini, rinse them to remove grit, and chop them.
3. Strain the soaking liquid through a non-bleached paper towel or cheesecloth-lined sieve; reserve for later.
4. Heat the olive oil in a saucepan. When hot, add the chopped soaked Porcini and saute for a few seconds.
5. Add the fresh mushrooms and cover; cook over very low heat until mushrooms are tender (if they begin to stick to the bottom of the pan, add a few spoonfuls of mushroom soaking liquid).
6. Add the reserved Porcini liquid and broth and bring to a simmer; add tortellini and cook, uncovered for 5 minutes or until tender.
7. Season to taste with salt and pepper and portion out; garnish with chives.
NOTE - To soak mushrooms in advance: Cover 2 ounces of dried mushrooms with 1 quart of water; set in refrigerator overnight or for longer. Remove soaked mushrooms and soaking liquid as needed.
